meta.title: CJNG Cartel Retaliatory Violence — Nationwide, February 22–23, 2026
meta.subtitle: 252 narcobloqueos across 20+ states following the killing of "El Mencho" in Tapalpa, Jalisco

infoBox.vehicleTypes: Trucks, buses, taxis, cars, Oxxo stores, banks, government buildings
infoBox.status: 90% cleared by Sunday evening; all cleared by Monday AM
infoBox.coordNote: 55–70+ dead (25 National Guard, 30 CJNG, civilians). 70+ arrested. 7,500 soldiers deployed to Jalisco.

incidents.title: Tapalpa — Military operation kills El Mencho
incidents.detail: Site of the military operation that killed Nemesio Oseguera Cervantes ("El Mencho"). Firefight between military forces and CJNG. 4 CJNG killed at scene, 3 wounded including Oseguera who died during airlift to Mexico City. CIA provided intelligence support.

incidents.title: Guadalajara — Ghost town, widespread burnings
incidents.detail: City turned into ghost town. Widespread vehicle burnings, road blockades at multiple points. Gas station set on fire. Gunfire at Guadalajara International Airport. Public transit fully suspended. CJNG circulated threat messages telling residents to stay indoors.

incidents.title: Zapopan — Fuel tanker burned, SAT offices attacked
incidents.detail: Fuel tanker burned on highway to Saltillo and Periférico Norte. SAT (tax) offices burned. Multiple Oxxo stores burned.

incidents.title: Puerto Vallarta — Fires, shootouts, executions
incidents.detail: Multiple fires on streets and highways. Vehicles burned in Costco parking lot. Execution-style killings near airport. Narco-blockades with spike strips. Shootouts between cartel gunmen and private security. Autozone store burned. Shelter-in-place ordered.

incidents.title: Ciudad Guzmán — Blockades and vehicle burnings
incidents.detail: Partial blockades and vehicle burnings at highway junctions.

incidents.title: Chapala — Narcobloqueos and shootings
incidents.detail: Narcobloqueos and shootings on highway from Guadalajara. Citizens warned others not to travel there.

incidents.title: Tecolotlán — Road blockades and burnings
incidents.detail: Road blockades and vehicle burnings on southern highway.

incidents.title: Mascota — Road blockade
incidents.detail: Road blockades on southern highway toward Puerto Vallarta.

incidents.title: Tlajomulco de Zúñiga — Oxxo stores burned
incidents.detail: Multiple Oxxo convenience stores burned.

incidents.title: Penal de Ixtapa — Prison break
incidents.detail: Prison break reported at the Ixtapa penal facility near Puerto Vallarta.

incidents.title: León — 18 arson incidents
incidents.detail: 18 arson incidents reported by noon. Coordinated attacks on businesses, Oxxo stores, and Farmacias Guadalajara.

incidents.title: Guanajuato capital — Arson attacks
incidents.detail: Arson attacks on commercial establishments in the state capital.

incidents.title: Irapuato — Incendiary attacks
incidents.detail: Incendiary attacks on businesses reported.

incidents.title: Silao — Incendiary attacks
incidents.detail: Incendiary attacks on businesses reported.

incidents.title: San Francisco del Rincón — Oxxo firebombed
incidents.detail: Oxxo on Bulevar Las Torres (Colonia Las Arboledas) firebombed with molotov cocktail.

incidents.title: Pénjamo — Armed confrontations
incidents.detail: Armed confrontations between gunmen and security forces.

incidents.title: Cointzio — Vehicles burned to block roads
incidents.detail: Charred vehicles set on fire to block roads. Widely photographed by media.

incidents.title: Zitácuaro — Road blockades, "overwhelming fear"
incidents.detail: Residents reported 'overwhelming fear.' Road blockades throughout the municipality.

incidents.title: Numarán — Trailer burned on highway
incidents.detail: Trailer set on fire on the Zináparo–La Piedad highway.

incidents.title: Jiquilpan — Vehicle burned, government building shot at
incidents.detail: Vehicle burned as road blockade. Gunmen shot at the municipal government building.

incidents.title: Churintzio — Truck driver forced out, vehicle burned
incidents.detail: Armed men forced a truck driver out of his cab and burned the vehicle.

incidents.title: Zamora region — Highway blockades
incidents.detail: Blockades on Zamora–La Barca highway and on access roads to La Piedad.

incidents.title: Buenavista–Tepalcatepec — Highway blockades
incidents.detail: Blockades near Santa Ana and Puente de Fierro on the highway.

incidents.title: Cancún — 3 vehicles burned, city entrance shut down
incidents.detail: Western entrance to city shut down after 3 vehicles burned on Mérida highway near Gas Imperial gas station. Armed men forced drivers out at gunpoint and burned vehicles. Massive security deployment by Marines, National Guard, and Civil Protection.

incidents.title: Playa del Carmen — Cars burned at Centro Maya
incidents.detail: 2 cars burned in Centro Maya shopping center parking lot. Another vehicle burned outside a private residence near Plaza Las Américas. Men on motorcycles reported setting fires.

incidents.title: Tulum — Oxxo stores & vehicles burned
incidents.detail: 2 Oxxo stores burned. 3 vehicles burned on outskirt highways. Vehicle fires on Tulum–Cobá highway forced road closure.

incidents.title: Cozumel — Bodega Aurrerá & Oxxo burned
incidents.detail: Fire at Bodega Aurrerá (Colonia Maravilla). Oxxo burned (Colonia Altamar). One arrest made.

incidents.title: San Francisco de los Romo — Trailer hijacked & burned
incidents.detail: Trailer hijacked and set on fire on Carretera 45 Norte, km 22.

incidents.title: Aguascalientes — Banco del Bienestar burned
incidents.detail: Banco del Bienestar branch set on fire in the city.

incidents.title: Puebla–Orizaba highway — Trailer burned
incidents.detail: Trailer set on fire on the Puebla–Orizaba autopista near Nogales. Highway blockade.

incidents.title: Santa Rita Tlahuapan — Truck burned
incidents.detail: Truck burned on the México–Puebla highway. Highway blockade.

incidents.title: Colima–La Cumbre highway — 2 vehicles burned
incidents.detail: Two vehicles set on fire on the highway toward Pihuamo.

incidents.title: Armería — Vehicle burning on highway bridge
incidents.detail: Total road closure due to vehicle burning on highway bridge at Libramiento Armería–Manzanillo, km 303+800.

incidents.title: Villa de Álvarez–Minatitlán — 3 vehicles burned
incidents.detail: Three vehicles set on fire at separate points on the carretera.

incidents.title: Tecomán / Cerro de Ortega — Vehicle burned
incidents.detail: Vehicle burned on carretera toward Playa Azul, Michoacán.

incidents.title: Reynosa — Massive blockades, buses hijacked & burned
incidents.detail: Blockades with burned vehicles and tires at city entrances. Armed men hijacked public transit buses and cargo trucks, forcing passengers out, then burning vehicles as barricades. Colonias Cumbres, Las Urracas, and Puerta del Sol affected. Libramiento a Monterrey blocked.

incidents.title: Nuevo Laredo — Blockades and vehicle fires
incidents.detail: Blockades and vehicles set on fire reported on the U.S. border.

incidents.title: Tepic — All commerce shut down, vehicles burned citywide
incidents.detail: All commerce shut down spontaneously. Streets completely emptied. Armed men with radios forced civilians out of cars and burned them throughout the city. Governor urged all residents to stay home.

incidents.title: Xalisco / Tepic–Xalisco boulevard — 4+ vehicles burned
incidents.detail: At least 4 vehicles set on fire — 2 cars and 2 trucks burned on the lane toward Puerto Vallarta and Compostela. Vehicles burned near Universidad Tecnológica.

incidents.title: Tepic–Mazatlán highway — Total road closure
incidents.detail: Total road closure due to vehicle burnings on the highway toward Mazatlán.

incidents.title: Acapulco — FGE building set on fire, buses burned
incidents.detail: State Attorney General's Office (FGE) building set on fire in Colonia Emiliano Zapata, 2 people injured. Two public transit buses burned. One public-service van burned. Flights delayed ~1 hour at Acapulco International Airport.

incidents.title: Autopista del Sol — Caseta La Venta blockade
incidents.detail: Tráiler used to blockade the highway at the toll booth. Total road closure on the major highway connecting Acapulco to Mexico City.

incidents.title: Zihuatanejo — 8-10+ vehicles burned
incidents.detail: 8-10+ vehicles burned across the municipality. Cargo trucks, transit Urvans, private cars, motorcycle. All commerce shut down. Las Dos Costas transit company HQ raided. See detailed Zihuatanejo map for individual incidents.

incidents.title: Tijuana — Taxi burned on Avenida Internacional
incidents.detail: Taxi intercepted and set on fire on Avenida Internacional. Zone secured by joint force of Municipal Police, Guardia Nacional, and Ejército.

incidents.title: Tecate — Tráiler burned, gunfire on highway
incidents.detail: Tráiler set on fire on Autopista Tecate–El Hongo producing massive smoke columns. Gunfire on carretera libre simultaneously. Vehicle burned near toll booth on Tecate–Mexicali highway.

incidents.title: San Quintín — Vehicle burnings on highway
incidents.detail: Vehicle burnings on highway reported by the governor.

incidents.title: Villa Unión, Mazatlán — 2 tráilers burned, bus hijacked
incidents.detail: Two tráilers set on fire on Bulevar Niños Héroes blocking all entry/exit. A passenger bus (Autotransportes UAS) hijacked with passengers aboard — passengers evacuated, bus burned. Two journalists from Noroeste beaten and held at gunpoint.

incidents.title: Escuinapa — Autopista blockade
incidents.detail: Blockade on autopista Mazatlán–Tepic using burned vehicle in the Tecualilla area. Cleared by security forces.

incidents.title: Tihuatlán–Álamo — Cargo trucks burned
incidents.detail: Armed men intercepted and burned cargo trucks on the federal highway in northern Veracruz.

incidents.title: Orizaba–Córdoba–Fortín corridor — Semi-trucks burned
incidents.detail: Tractocamiones (semi-trucks) set on fire and used to block Autopista 150D. Dense smoke column near Fortín de las Flores.

incidents.title: Coatzacoalcos–Villahermosa — Cargo vehicles burned
incidents.detail: Two cargo vehicles consumed by fire on the highway at Ejido 5 de Mayo. Additional tractocamiones burned near Agua Dulce.

incidents.title: Monterrey–Reynosa highway — Attempted blockade
incidents.detail: Attempted blockade at Tamaulipas border. Dismantled within minutes by Fuerza Civil. 'Operativo Muralla' activated statewide.

incidents.title: Zacatecas–Guadalajara highway — Blockades and burnings
incidents.detail: Total road closure near km 224 due to vehicle burnings and narcobloqueos. Banco del Bienestar branches attacked and burned in municipalities bordering Jalisco.

incidents.title: Tula de Allende — Vehicles burned
incidents.detail: Vehicles set on fire. One of 6 municipalities in Hidalgo affected.

incidents.title: Tepeji del Río — Vehicles burned
incidents.detail: Vehicles set on fire. Schools suspended across the region.

incidents.title: Circuito Exterior Mexiquense — Vehicle burned as blockade
incidents.detail: Vehicle set on fire and used to block one of the most important beltways connecting northern Valle de México municipalities at km 19 near Ecatepec/Coacalco/Tultitlán corridor.

incidents.title: Temixco — Taxi and store burned
incidents.detail: Taxi set on fire. A commercial establishment (tienda) attacked and burned.

incidents.title: Jiutepec — Convenience store burned
incidents.detail: Convenience store set on fire by two armed men in Colonia Miguel Hidalgo. Perpetrators identified, arrest operation launched.

incidents.title: Ixtacuixtla–Atotonilco highway — Vehicle burned
incidents.detail: Two men arrived, positioned a vehicle across the road, set it on fire, and fled at Comunidad Espíritu Santo near Puebla border.

incidents.title: Caseta de Palmillas — Toll booth closed
incidents.detail: Toll booth closed by CAPUFE due to security concerns. UNAM suspended activities at Querétaro campus.

incidents.title: Caseta de Lagos de Moreno — Toll booth closed
incidents.detail: Toll booth closed. Toll road operations temporarily curtailed.

incidents.title: Istmo de Tehuantepec — Schools suspended
incidents.detail: Schools suspended on Monday Feb 23. Isolated blockades cleared quickly.

shutdowns.title: Jalisco statewide — 81 Oxxo stores, 18 banks burned
shutdowns.detail: 81 Oxxo stores burned, 18 Banco del Bienestar branches damaged. Chivas match canceled. All schools suspended. Churches canceled masses. 25 National Guard troops killed in 6 attacks.

shutdowns.title: Guanajuato statewide — 60+ arson attacks in 23 municipalities
shutdowns.detail: 60+ arson attacks across 23 of 46 municipalities. Oxxo stores, Farmacias Guadalajara, and public transit buses burned.

disruptions.title: Lázaro Cárdenas — Port & Transit Disruption
disruptions.detail: C5 Michoacán confirmed traffic disruptions. SAT office closed. DHL reported reduced freight volumes. Key Pacific port for fentanyl precursor chemicals.
